# Unofficial Meeting Notes
## Conroe Independent School District Board of Trustees — Regular Meeting
### June 19, 2018

> **Note:** These are **unofficial notes** based on an auto-generated transcript of the board meeting. They are intended to help the public understand what occurred and may contain minor errors due to transcript quality.

---

## Opening
- Meeting called to order at 6:00 PM by President Bush.
- A quorum was confirmed; proper notice was posted per the Texas Open Meetings Act.
- Mr. Husbands led the invocation; President Bush led the Pledge of Allegiance and Texas Pledge.

---

## Special Board Recognitions (Items 2A–2H)

### 2A — UIL Class 6A Girls' 3,200 Meter State Champion
- **Sophie Laswald**, Oak Ridge High School, was recognized as the 2018 UIL Class 6A Girls' 3,200 Meter State Champion with a time of **10:24**.
- She was also district, area, and regional champion, winning every 3,200 meter race during the season.
- Introduced by Oak Ridge Principal **Tommy Johnson** and head track coach **Sterling Beck** (first year at Oak Ridge).

### 2B–2E — The Woodlands High School Boys' Track & Field – 2018 6A State Champions
- Presented by former Woodlands HS Principal **Greg Colschen** and head boys' track coach **Juris Green**.
- The team qualified in **8 events with 9 scoring opportunities**; all 9 scored at the state meet, accumulating **68 points** for the school's **22nd state title**.
- Key accomplishments highlighted:
  - **Keyshawn Carter**: Back-to-back 100m state champion (10.22 seconds)
  - **4x100m relay**: 5th fastest time in U.S. high school history (39.98)
  - **4x200m relay**: National record (1:23.25)
  - Additional placings in discus, shot put, 3,200m, high jump, and 1,600m
- Several athletes were absent due to college orientations/football prep.
- Athletes **Patrick Piperi** and **Shawn Stevenoa** (throwers) were present.
- Coaching staff recognized: Shawn Hamilton, Chris Bales, Gary Madore, Mike Tolliver, and Robbie Dueitt.

### 2E — The Woodlands High School Girls' Discus State Champion
- **Nya Harmon**, sophomore, was recognized as the 6A girls' discus state champion. She was unable to attend.

### 2F — Division One Lacrosse State Champions — The Woodlands High School
- The Woodlands HS lacrosse team won its **first-ever state championship** in the program's 21-year history.
- Coach **Keith Tintle** noted the team defeated three Dallas-area teams to win the title, breaking a 12-year streak of Dallas school dominance.
  - Semifinal: beat Highland Park 10–6
  - Championship: beat St. Mark's 10–6
- The team had **10 all-state players**, **13 district award winners**, and **3 all-Americans**.
- Program founder **David Seal** (CISD staff member) was acknowledged but unable to attend.
- Players and assistant coach **Al Christopher** were recognized.

### 2G — Maintenance & Custodial Ambassador of the Year Awards
- Presented by **Marshall Schroeder**, Director of Custodial Maintenance.
- Award recipients:
  - **Elio Yanez** — North Maintenance, athletic groundsman (since 1999)
  - **Michael Cowell** — South Maintenance, journeyman electrician (since 2015)
  - **Li Sim Chi** — South Custodial, Kaufman Elementary swing shift (since 2008)
  - **Maria Ayala** — North Custodial, Giesinger Elementary opener (since 2000)
  - **Julian Guna** — Assistant Director Wildcard, leading crew lead (since 1996)
  - **Jimmy Atkinson** — Director's Wildcard, Lamar Elementary (since 1985); described as "a legend at Lamar" with a large cheering section present
- President Bush praised the maintenance and custodial teams, noting that CISD facilities are "on-par with any ivy league college."

### 2H — National Integrated Pest Management (IPM) Star Associate Certification
- Presented by **Dr. Chris Hines** and **Janet Hurley** (Extension Specialist, Texas A&M AgriLife Extension Service).
- Conroe ISD is **one of only four districts in Texas** to receive the IPM Star Certification from the IPM Institute of North America based on a **37-point evaluation**.
- IPM team members recognized: **John Brown** (maintenance coordinator), **Ray Brown** (IPM coordinator), **Keith Murphy** (pesticide applicator), **Gilbert Pochako** (pesticide applicator).
- **Dr. Paul Nester** was also acknowledged for assisting with pest issues (crazy ants and fire ants).
- Ms. Hurley provided historical context: Conroe ISD adopted IPM practices as early as 1989 (before the 1995 state law), initially prompted by parent **Rebecca Parella** whose daughter was chemically sensitive. The district became a state model.
- The district manages IPM for **54 campuses, 14 additional facilities, and 55 athletic fields**.

---

## Public Comments (Item 3 — Public Participation)

### Amy Hamerick — Harper's Landing resident, parent of twins at Powell Elementary
- Spoke **against arming teachers and staff** in Conroe ISD schools.
- Referenced Governor Abbott's Texas School and Firearm Safety Action Plan and the school marshal program.
- Urged the board to **formally reject the school marshal plan** and issue a resolution against arming teachers.
- Cited FBI data on active shooter interventions, research on gun presence increasing risk, and opposition from the American Federation of Teachers, National Education Association, and Major Cities Chiefs Association.
- Also urged the board to recommend red flag laws to the governor.

### Dr. Enrique Rosaro — Parent and community member
- Expressed concern about **declining state funding** for public education (down to 38% per student from 49% a decade ago).
- Noted the district appears to rely on increasing property appraisals at ~6% per year, which he called "unsustainable."
- Referenced a recent failed tax ratification election at Klein ISD.
- Urged the board to **engage with state legislators** to demand adequate school funding.
- Expressed concern about political candidates he characterized as "enemies of public education" who blame administrators for funding issues.
- Asked the board to refute what he called false statements about the district.

---

## Consent Agenda (Item 3)
- No items were pulled for separate discussion.
- **Vote: Approved as presented (unanimous/no opposition noted).**

---

## Action Items

### 4A — Application to TEA Hurricane Recovery Federal Grants Program
- Presented by **Dr. Chris Hines**.
- The board was asked to authorize the district to apply for federal grant funding related to **Hurricane Harvey** costs.
- Three anticipated grants:
  1. **Hurricane Restart Grant** — potential allocation up to **$2.7 million**; covers costs from August 2017 forward for two years; includes both reimbursement and forward-looking services.
  2. **Impact Aid for Displaced Students**
  3. **Homeless Children and Youth Program**
- Grant details are still unfolding; no matching requirements identified yet for the first grant.
- The district finished the year with approximately **1,000 homeless students**, a significant increase attributed in part to hurricane displacement.
- Plans include potential additional positions (grant-funded with two-year limit), needs assessments, counseling, behavioral training, transportation, and health services support.
- **Vote: Approved (unanimous/no opposition noted).**

### 4B — Acceptance of 2016 CTE Robotics Project & Final Payment Authorization
- Presented by **Mr. Foster**.
- Project established **welding, construction trades, cosmetology, and robotics** programs at Caney Creek High School and expanded welding classes at Oak Ridge 9th Grade Campus.
- Project is complete and has been in use for two school years.
- Board members praised the CTE programs and noted **substantial savings** compared to original cost estimates.
- **Vote: Approved (unanimous/no opposition noted).**

### 4C — Acceptance of 2016 Life Cycle Project & Final Payment Authorization
- Presented by **Mr. Foster**.
- Major work included:
  - Complete HVAC/mechanical systems upgrade at **Runyan Elementary**
  - Full roof replacement at **Caney Creek High School**
  - Turf replacements, wood floor work, stadium track improvements district-wide
- Project is complete.
- **Vote: Approved (unanimous/no opposition noted).**

## Financial Items

### 5A — Approval of 2018–2022 Annual Financial Audit Proposal
- Presented by **Mr. Rice**.
- Proposal extends the relationship with **Weaver and Tidwell** for professional audit services for **five additional years**.
- Audit fee: **$85,000/year** for the first two years, increasing by 1.5% annually to **$90,000** by the end of the term.
- The board's **Audit Committee voted unanimously** to recommend approval.
- Board member (audit committee chair) praised the working relationship with Weaver and Tidwell and credited Mr. Rice's team and internal auditor Ms. Keenan for helping keep audit costs down.
- **Vote: Approved (unanimous/no opposition noted).**

Presented by **Mr. Rice**:

**General Fund — Cash & Investments:**
- Cash on hand: $500
- Bank deposits: $102,000
- State pool investments: $92.9 million
- Woodforest National Bank (new): $30 million (25 basis points above 90-day T-bill, collateralized)
- Short-term investments (<1 year): $89 million
- Longer-term investments (TCG): $51.7 million
- **Total cash and investments: $263.8 million**

**Projected Fund Balances:**
- General fund: projected increase of ~**$1.6 million**
- Child nutrition: projected increase has decreased from prior month due to approved kitchen construction costs

**Self-Funded Insurance (May):**
- Revenue: $4.0 million; Expenses: $5.2 million
- 4 high claims; 3 breached $500,000 stop-loss threshold (including 2 premature babies and loss of a teacher's spouse)
- Monthly deficit: ~$1.2 million
- **Year-to-date**: Revenue $36+ million; Expenses $33.4 million; **Net positive ~$2.6 million**

**Wellness Center:** 475 visits in May; 4,713 year-to-date

**Investment Portfolio:**
- Total par value: $560 million
- Combined portfolio yield: 1.59% (53-day WAM)
- Benchmark (90-day T-bill): 1.89%
